INVME_6306_357_V1.0(MLE): -Added Auto ident 6306_3560 VDiag $04; -Added Auto ident 6306_3570 VDiag $04; -Modified Supplier field in Auto-identification
INVME_6306_354_V1.0 (ARA): -Added Auto ident 6306_354 VDiag $04 -Updated DID List
INVME_6306_352_V1.0 (ARA): -Added Auto ident 6306_354 VDiag $04 -Added Screen "PVALCOP VP";
INVME_6306_350_V1.0 (ARA): -Added Auto ident 6306_350 VDiag $04 -Added Auto ident 6306_346 VDiag $04 Modified DID $7032 Added DIDs $70FE,$F18E

INVME_6306_344_V1.0(SMA): -Updated DTC List;
INVME_6306_341_V1.2(SMA): -Added Auto ident 6306_342_343_344_345 VDiag $04
INVME_6306_341_V1.1(CDI): -Corrected DID $704B, $70D0, $70D9, $70E2, $70E3, $70E4, $70E5, $70E6, $7404, $7405, $F40C, $F40D, $F411, $F442, $F447, $F451, $F45B -Updated DIDs: $FD6F; $2004; $7002; $7006; $700C; $7012; $7022; $7023; $7025; $7026; $7029; $7031; $7039; $703A; $703C; $7043; $7044; $7049; $708E; $709E; $709F; $70A1; $70A2; $70A3; $70B3; -Added DIDs: $FDD7, $FDD8, $FDD9, $FDDA, $FDDB, $FDDC -Removed "official" from all the DIDs EOL; -Added DID $2073 to screen "Verrou logiciel"
INVME_6306_341_V1.0(CDI): -Added Auto ident 6306_341 VDiag $04 -Rename screen "Resistance-Zero angle" to "Zero angle" -Delete "ROTOR RESISTANCE" from "Zero angle" screen -Added DTCs: $0A1C; $0A30; $0A3D; $0A45; $0A55; $0A79; $0A91; $0BCC; $0BFE; $0C02; $0C04; $0C08; $0C0E; $0C55; $0E43; $1A20; $1A21; $1A22; $1A24; $1A26; $1A27; $1A28; $1A29; $1A2A; $1A2B; $1A2C; $1A2D; $1A2E; $1A2F; $1A41; $1A42; $1A43; $1A44; $1A45; $1A46; $1A47; $1A48; $1A49; $1A60; $1A61; $C112; $C292; $C293; $C299; $C594; $E140; $E142; $E144; $E157;
INVME_6306_340_V2.0(AJO): Added Auto ident 6306_340 VDiag $04 Added DIDs $70D7 $70D5 $70D6 $70A3 $70A4 $70A5 Modified DID $F180 Renamed DTCs conform DTC_base_VE
INVME_6306_340_V1.0(AJO): Added Auto ident 6306_340 VDiag $14 Added DIDs $70F6 $70F7 $70F8 $70F5 $F80F $F421 $70F9 $70FA $70FB $70FC $70FD $70F4 Modified DIDs $700D $F430 $F431 $706E Update screen Routine UDS Modified DID $F180 Ident BOOT Update DTC list

INVME_6306_330_V1.0 -Added Auto ident 6306_330 VDiag $14 -Removed "Rotor copper temperature estimation (°C)" from screen "Traction-Charge" -Added applications XJA, XJB -Added routine $0013 "Position sensor learning" -Corrected DTC name -Modified DID $70DE -Added DIDs $F802 $F804 $F806 $F80A $70F3 -Added requests $1906 88 and 89

Item.Text: Nsx_hv_nok (0): More than one sensor failure
Nsx_hv_dcdc_dfl (3): HV DCDC voltage sensor Failure
Nsx_hv_bat_dfl (4): HV Battery voltage sensor Failure
Nsx_hv_nw_loss : HV Network Loss or false relay status information
Nsx_hv_bat_sfty_dfl (6): HV battery safety mode 1 (cell or pack overvoltage or safety request)
Nsx_hv_rly_open_dfl: false open relay status information
Nsx_stc_uex_brak : "Unexpected braking" is detected by the "static control strategy"
Nsx_stc_uex_acc_neg : "Unexpected acceleration" in case of negative torque request is detected by the "static control strategy"
Nsx_stc_uex_acc : "Unexpected acceleration" is detected by the "static control strategy"
Nsx_dyn_uex_acc : "Unexpected acceleration" is detected by the "dynamic control strategy"
Nsx_stc_obrak : "Over braking" is detected by the "static control strategy"
Nsx_dyn_obrak : "Over braking" or "Unexpected braking" are detected by the "dynamic control strategy"
Nsx_stc_lack_motr : "Lack of motricity" is detected by the "static control strategy"
Nsx_dyn_lack_motr : "Lack of motricity" is detected by the "dynamic control strategy"
Nsx_stc_rege_tq_loss : "Loss of regenerative torque" is detected by the "static control strategy"
Nsx_dyn_rege_tq_loss : "Loss of regenerative torque" is detected by the "dynamic control strategy"
Nsx_ov_der_dfl : Non zero generated torque detected in case of overvoltage
Nsx_term_der_dfl : Non limited torque detected in case of rotor overtemperature (rotor temperature = 100%)
Nsx_over_tq_max : state of over torque hign limit
Nsx_under_tq_min : state of under torque low limit

RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifier
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Display.RequestName: DataRead.FPGA firmware version Identifier

Label.Text: Si Etat protégé = 1 alors démarrage impossible
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
Danc ce cas le calculateur est déprotégé et n'interdit pas le démarrage moteur
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est protégé car il ne reçoit pas les trames de la BCM: l'ECM n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est protégé car la BCM ne répond pas correctement: l'ECM n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
-> La BCM n'a pas authentifié la clé ou le badge
-> L'ECM n'est pas monté sur le véhicule sur lequel il a appris le code
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est vierge et protégé car la BCM n'envoie pas la clé secrète: l'ECM n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
-> La BCM n'est pas autorisée à envoyer la clé secrète
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est "autoprotégé" et n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
-> L'ECM a subit une tentative "d'intrusion" ou a détécté une erreur interne (EEPROM, FLASH...)
Bit5/Bit4 : 0=ECM virgin - Secret key not learnt / 1=Not used / 2=Not virgin ECM - Secret key learnt in erasable area / 3=Not virgin ECM - Secret key learnt in non erasable area
Bit0 : 0=Immobilizer engine running forbidden / 1=Immobilizer engine running authorized
